title: Ovulation Calculator 2026 — Fertile Window & Menstrual Cycle | Hacé Cuentas
h1: Ovulation and Menstrual Cycle Calendar Calculator
description: Ovulation calculator: enter your menstrual cycle and calculate your fertile days, ovulation date, and fertility window. Free and instant.
intro: Knowing your **fertile days** is key whether you're trying to conceive or want to avoid it. Ovulation occurs approximately **14 days before your next period**, and the **fertile window** spans the **5 days before ovulation + the day of ovulation** (6 days total). This calculator uses your LMP (last menstrual period) and cycle length to estimate those dates.
keyTakeaway: The **fertile window** lasts about **6 days**: sperm survive up to 5 days, and the egg lives 12–24 hours. The most fertile day is the **day before ovulation**.
useCases: You're trying to conceive and want to know your most fertile days.
You use the calendar method as a complement to contraception.
You want to plan intercourse to maximize the chances of conception.
Your OB/GYN asked you to record your cycle and ovulation dates.
You want to better understand your menstrual cycle and its phases.

fields.label: First day of your last period
Cycle length (days)
fields.help: Count from the first day of one period to the first day of the next

example.title: Example: LMP April 1, 28-day cycle
steps: **Fertile window**: **April 10 to April 15** (5 days before + day of ovulation).
example.result: Your ovulation is approximately **April 15**. Your fertile window runs from **April 10 to April 15**. Next period: **April 29**.

explanation:
## How Ovulation Is Calculated

Ovulation occurs approximately **14 days before the next period** (luteal phase). This is fairly constant regardless of cycle length:

```
Ovulation day = Cycle length − 14
```

For example:
- 28-day cycle → ovulation day 14
- 30-day cycle → ovulation day 16
- 26-day cycle → ovulation day 12

## The Fertile Window

**Sperm** can survive in the female reproductive tract up to **5 days**. The **egg** is viable only **12–24 hours** after ovulation. This creates a ~6-day window:

| Day relative to ovulation | Pregnancy probability |
|---|---|
| −5 days | ~5% |
| −4 days | ~10% |
| −3 days | ~15% |
| −2 days | ~25% |
| −1 day (most fertile) | ~30% |
| Ovulation day | ~12% |
| +1 day | ~0% (egg no longer viable) |

## Signs of Ovulation

- **Cervical mucus**: becomes clear and stretchy (like egg white).
- **Basal body temperature**: rises ~0.3–0.5°C after ovulation.
- **Ovulation pain (Mittelschmerz)**: mild discomfort on one side of the lower abdomen.
- **Ovulation test (LH)**: detects the LH hormone surge 24–36 hours before.

## Limitations of the Calendar Method

This calculation assumes **regular cycles**. If your cycles vary by more than 3–4 days, prediction loses accuracy. For irregular cycles, supplement with basal body temperature tracking and LH tests.

faq.q: How long does the fertile window last?
faq.a: The fertile window lasts approximately **6 days**: the 5 days before ovulation + the day of ovulation. The day with the highest chance of conception is the **day before ovulation** (~30% probability).

faq.q: How do I know how long my menstrual cycle is?
faq.a: Count from the **first day of bleeding** of one period to the **first day of bleeding** of the next. Do this for 3–6 months and take the average. A normal cycle lasts between **21 and 35 days**.

faq.q: Can I get pregnant outside the fertile window?
faq.a: It's **very unlikely** but not impossible, especially if your cycles are irregular and ovulation happens at a different time than estimated. Sperm can survive up to 5 days, so an inaccurate calculation could miss fertile days.

The calendar method (Ogino-Knaus) has **75–88% effectiveness** with typical use, well below modern methods (pill 91%, IUD 99%). **It is not recommended as a primary contraceptive method**. Consult your OB/GYN.

faq.q: When is the best time to have sex if I want to conceive?
faq.a: The **2–3 days before ovulation** are the most fertile. Ideally, have sex **every 1–2 days throughout the fertile window** (5 days before ovulation through the day of ovulation).

faq.q: Does the calculator work with irregular cycles?
faq.a: With irregular cycles the estimate is less precise. If your cycle varies by more than 5 days between one and the next, combine the calendar method with basal temperature and LH tests.

faq.q: What is the luteal phase and how long does it last?
faq.a: The **luteal phase** is the period between ovulation and the next period. It normally lasts **12–16 days** (average 14). It's fairly constant in each woman. A luteal phase shorter than 10 days can indicate a hormonal issue that makes implantation difficult.

sources.name: ACOG — Fertility Awareness-Based Methods of Family Planning
Wilcox AJ et al. — Timing of sexual intercourse in relation to ovulation (NEJM, 1995)

howToSteps.name: Enter your LMP
howToSteps.text: Select the first day of your last menstrual period.

howToSteps.name: Enter your cycle length
howToSteps.text: Enter how many days your cycle lasts (e.g., 28). If unsure, use 28 as the average.

howToSteps.name: Click Calculate
howToSteps.text: Press Calculate to see your ovulation date and fertile window.

howToSteps.name: Review the results
howToSteps.text: You'll see the ovulation date, fertile window, and next estimated period.
